VAL-COMPAT-001.evidence: compat_report.json shows all 8 scenarios PASS with zero divergent lines. Scenarios: (1) Simple String Match, (2) Case-Insensitive Match, (3) Regex Match, (4) Invert Match, (5) Count Matches (rg_count=800002, tg_count=800002), (6) Context Lines, (7) Max Count Limit, (8) File Glob Filtering. All exit codes match. Path separator normalization (backslash to forward slash) applied via normalize_lines(). sorted-set comparison used for line-based scenarios; exact integer equality for --count.
VAL-COMPAT-001.details: run_compat_checks.py executed from repo root. All 8 benchmark scenarios produced PASS with zero missing_lines and zero extra_lines. Count scenario confirmed exact integer equality (800002 == 800002). Exit codes matched between rg and tg for all scenarios. Script normalizes path separators to '/' before sorted-set comparison. Full report at artifacts/compat_report.json. pytest gate: 503 passed, 14 skipped.

VAL-COMPAT-002.evidence: tg.exe --json ERROR bench_data emits valid JSON: {"routing_backend":"CpuBackend","routing_reason":"cpu-native","sidecar_used":false,"query":"ERROR","path":"...","total_matches":800002}. tg.exe --verbose emits '[routing] routing_backend=CpuBackend routing_reason=cpu-native sidecar_used=false' on stderr. JSON validates against tests/schemas/tg_output.schema.json schema (jsonschema.validate() passes). Schema requires routing_backend, routing_reason, sidecar_used, query, path, total_matches with enum constraints.
VAL-COMPAT-002.details: JSON output from tg.exe --json contains all required fields: routing_backend='CpuBackend', routing_reason='cpu-native', sidecar_used=false, query='ERROR', path=bench_data, total_matches=800002. Schema validation passed (jsonschema library). Verbose output emits structured routing metadata on stderr. Schema file committed at tests/schemas/tg_output.schema.json with additionalProperties:false and enum constraints on routing_backend and routing_reason.
